title: Show HN: Vanilla JavaScript refinery simulator built to explain job to my kids
full_text:
Did You Know?

⚙️ Interactive STEM Adventure

 

The Great
Refinery Run

 

From Crude to Consumer —
guide oil through every step of the refinery!

 

 🛢️
 →
 🏭
 →
 ⛽
 →
 🚛
 

 🚀 Start the Run!
 

 

 

By Taylor Bloomquist
 

© 2026 Taylor Bloomquist · All rights reserved
 

Inspired by Fueling Curiosity: The ABCs of Refining
 

 

Phase 1: Extraction

 

 

Tap the pump jack, the tank, or the button to extract crude oil!

 Pump to Refinery! (0/5)

 

Phase 1b: The Desalter
 

Crude oil has salty water in it! Zap the salt 🧂 and water 💧 before they escape the grid! But don't zap the oil ⚫️! 
 
 

 Grid powering up...
 

 

 
 To Distillation Tower!
 Restart Desalter

 

Phase 2: Distillation
 

Inside the tower, crude oil gets heated and separates by weight! Choose a product path:

 

 

 

 LPG 💨
(Top - Lightest)
 Naphtha 🧪
 Jet Fuel ✈️
 Diesel 🚛
 Resid 🪨
(Bottom - Heaviest)
 
 

 

 

Phase 3: Hydrotreating
 

We need to clean the sulfur out!
 

 Send to Processing!

 

HF Alkylation Unit
 

Tap the 🫧 molecules in the emulsion layer to make Alkylate! Warning: Drag the dark tar (ASO) 🟤 to the Acid Regen box before acid purity drops below 80%!
 
 

 Acid Purity: 100%
 Alkylate: 0/4
 

 

 

 

Hydrocarbon
 

Acid/HC Emulsion
 

Settled HF Acid
 
 
 

 
 

🔥 Acid Regen
 

 
 To Gasoline Blender!
 Restart Alky

 

Catalytic Reformer
 

Naphtha is low octane! 🔥 the furnace to heat the straight molecules, release Hydrogen, and reshape them into powerful Ring ⬡ molecules!
 

 To Gasoline Blender!

 

Vacuum Tower
 

Resid is too thick to distill normally! Remove the air pressure so the heavy oil boils at a lower temperature.
 

 

 

Vacuum Fractionator
 

 

 VGO (Vacuum Gas Oil)
 VTB (Vacuum Tower Bottoms)
 
 

 

 

Coker Fractionator
 

The hot vapors from the drum separate into cracked products! Choose one to process:
 

 

Fluid Catalytic Cracking (FCC)
 

VGO molecules 🟠 are too big to use as fuel! Tap them to CRACK them apart! Cracking causes coke buildup ⚫! Don't forget to regen your catalyst.
 

 

 

FCC Fractionator
 

Cracked molecules are separated into cracked products! Choose one to process:
 

 

 LPG 💨
 Gasoline 🚗
 ULSD 🚛
 

Slurry goes to sales!🛢️
 
 

 

 

Phase 4: Blending & Processing
 

 

 

Phase 5: Logistics
 

Your product is ready! How do you want to deliver it?
 

 

 
 🚛
 Tanker Truck
 ~9,000 gallons
 
 
 🚂
 Rail Car
 ~ 3 trucks/rail car
 
 
 🛳️
 Barge
 = 100 trucks
 
 
 🚢
 Ocean Tanker
 10+ million gallons
 
 
 🚰
 Pipeline
 Millions of gallons/day · Lowest cost per gallon
 
 

 

Mission Accomplished! 🥳 You did your part to contribute to the 630 million gallons of fuel the US needs EVERY day.
 

 

 

 

Keep the learning going!
 

Did you enjoy the demonstration? Discover more about how refineries power our world with the ABCs of Refining on Amazon.
 
 📚 Buy the Book on Amazon
 
 
 

 

Critical Alert: Pump Vibration Rising
 

 

🟢 Open Pump B suction, ▶️ start Pump B, 🟢 open Pump B discharge, then ⛔ isolate Pump A.
 
 Procedure
 
 

 

 
 
 

 Standby
 Ready but isolated
 
 

 

 

 To Fractionator
 
 

 

 

 

 

Pump A
 Primary online
 
 

Primary

 
 

 

 

 

Pump B

Standby

 
 
 

 

 From Feed Drum
 
 
 

 

 Control Room
 Awaiting operator action.
 

 
 Retry Swap
 

 

 

 

Standard Operating Procedure: Hot Swap
 
 
Open Spare Suction: Flood the spare pump with liquid to protect the mechanical seals.
 
Start Spare Motor: Spin up the spare pump against its closed discharge valve.
 
Open Spare Discharge: Establish forward flow through the spare pump.
 
Close Primary Discharge: Pinch off the bad pump to prevent fluid from slamming backward.
 
Stop Primary Motor: Shut down the vibrating equipment.
 
Close Primary Suction: Fully isolate the bad pump so maintenance can safely pull the casing.
 
 Acknowledge
 
 

 

Pipe X-Ray Maintenance
 

Incoming Diesel pipe integrity compromised! Scan for thin spots, tap to mark them, then switch to the clamp tool to fix them!
 
 

 

 Time to Failure: 45.0s
 Clamps: 0/3
 
 
 

 
 

 
 🔍 X-Ray Scanner
 
 
 🔧 Pipe Clamp
 
 

 
 

 Restart Maintenance

Tap a unit to jump. Green = certified ✅

🎓 V-804 Refinery Explorer Certificate
 

 You've completed all 15 units! Enter your name and choose your Explorer title.

 🖨️ Print Certificate
 
 

 

 
 

 

 Fueling Curiosity: The ABCs of Refining
 
 

 Taylor Bloomquist
 
 
 
 

 

 

About The Great Refinery Run
 

The Great Refinery Run is an interactive STEM demonstration designed to showcase the accessibility of the refining information available in the book Fueling Curiosity. In this interactive journey, users guide crude oil through several critical stages:
 
 
Phase 1: Extraction: Simulating a pumpjack pulling raw crude oil from the ground into a storage tank.
 
Phase 1b: Desalting: Removing salt and water from crude oil using electrostatic voltage.
 
Phase 2: Distillation: Heating the crude oil in a distillation tower to separate it into fractions like Gasoline, Jet Fuel, Diesel, and heavy Resid.
 
Phase 3: Hydrotreating: Removing sulfur impurities from the raw product through hydrotreating to make the fuel clean.
 
Phase 4: Processing and Blending: Depending on the product chosen, players visually blend components or purify fuels through unit-specific minigames.
 
Phase 5: Logistics: Learning how finished products safely reach consumers via Tanker Trucks, Railcars, River Barges, Ocean Tanker Ships, or underground Pipelines.
 
 

Please enable JavaScript in your browser to play the interactive simulation!
